reasoning: The Lean hypothesis `∀ v : V, ∃ c : F, v ∈ eigenspace S c` unfolds to `∀ v, ∃ c, S v = c • v`. Though Axler's convention excludes 0 from being an eigenvector, the v=0 case in the Lean formulation is trivially satisfied (0 lies in every eigenspace), so the hypothesis is logically equivalent to "every nonzero vector is an eigenvector of S." The conclusion `∃ c, S = c • LinearMap.id` correctly captures "S is a scalar multiple of the identity." The types match: `End F V` corresponds to L(V), and no spurious hypotheses (finite-dimensionality, characteristic, etc.) are imposed — the theorem holds over arbitrary fields. This is a faithful encoding.
